response.justification: The candidate has a lengthy software career but is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. C++ experience is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 writing components for a C IDE, and it does not appear in the candidate's own skills list. The primary stack throughout the career is JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js/DLang. There is no evidence of multi-threaded C++ systems, STL proficiency, MQTT, message brokers, industrial-automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443, or any cybersecurity engineering practice — all of which are central to this role.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with event-driven and distributed architectures (GISCollective platform, Sociomantic micro-services)
Consistent agile/craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development across multiple roles
Familiarity with REST APIs, Node.js, and React — covering some of the listed advantageous skills
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months, 2014–2015) and absent from the candidate's own skills inventory — the role demands it as the primary language
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or any OT/ICS domain exposure
No MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or automation/IoT engineering background
